title: loeselia glandulosa subsp. sonorae
intro: Loeselia glandulosa subsp. sonorae, also known as Arizona loeelia, is a plant species that belongs to the borage family and is native to the southwestern regions of North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Loeselia glandulosa subsp. sonorae include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, soothe skin irritations, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
